rows.next_action: Define paper-specific geometry/species parameters and fit digitized trajectory or response curves.
rows.title: Modeling tropotaxis in ant colonies: recruitment and trail formation
rows.gap: The model uses left/right/front sampling and exports per-step trajectory/sensing plus segment-flow metrics; exact tropotaxis equation fitting still needs digitized paper trajectories.

rows.next_action: Add active detractor/cautionary pheromone agents and calibrate attack/defense effect sizes.
rows.title: A Model for Foraging Ants, Controlled by Spiking Neural Networks and Double Pheromones
rows.gap: The simulator now pairs avoid pheromone with short-term individual avoid memory, but it is still an ABM rule rather than the paper's spiking-neural-controller implementation.

rows.next_action: Add digitized paper-level biological measurements and fit only shared model parameters, not paper-specific exception rules.
rows.title: A continuous model of ant foraging with pheromones and trail formation
rows.gap: Trail formation and decay are represented, but the simulator is an ABM with heuristic field units rather than Amorim's calibrated PDE chemotaxis variables.

rows.next_action: Add digitized paper-level biological measurements and fit only shared model parameters, not paper-specific exception rules.
rows.title: A stochastic model of ant trail following with two pheromones
rows.gap: The simulator separates food/nest/water fields, but it is not yet the exact two-pheromone mathematical model and lacks direct synchronization metrics.

rows.next_action: Calibrate trail geometry, body-contact/lane rules and digitized flow-density curves.
rows.title: Trafficlike collective movement of ants on trails: absence of a jammed phase
rows.gap: The validation now uses segment-level speed/flow-density metrics. It still lacks calibrated body-contact rules and digitized no-jam flow curves.

rows.next_action: Add digitized paper-level biological measurements and fit only shared model parameters, not paper-specific exception rules.
rows.title: Optimal and Resilient Pheromone Utilization in Ant Foraging
rows.gap: Afek et al. is an algorithmic pheromone model; this ABM only tests biological-style degradation, not asymptotic pheromone lower bounds or proof-level optimality.

rows.next_action: Add digitized paper-level biological measurements and fit only shared model parameters, not paper-specific exception rules.
rows.title: Optimal traffic organisation in ants under crowded conditions
rows.gap: The model now exports segment-level density, speed, flow and traffic-redirect probability. It still lacks calibrated body-contact/pushing mechanics from crowded trail experiments.

rows.next_action: Add active detractor/cautionary pheromone agents and calibrate attack/defense effect sizes.
rows.title: Hacking the Colony: On the Disruptive Effect of Misleading Pheromone and How to Defend Against It
rows.gap: The probe now uses sustained external fake-pheromone perturbation and generic avoid learning, but still lacks explicit attacker agents and calibrated attack/defense effect sizes.

rows.next_action: Add digitized paper-level biological measurements and fit only shared model parameters, not paper-specific exception rules.
rows.title: Diverse Stochasticity Leads a Colony of Ants to Optimal Foraging
rows.gap: The current check measures relative relocation adaptation. It does not yet fit the paper's environment-dependent optimal stochasticity distribution.

rows.next_action: Define paper-specific geometry/species parameters and fit digitized trajectory or response curves.
rows.title: Individual rules for trail pattern formation in Argentine ants (Linepithema humile)
rows.gap: Trail reinforcement, per-step trajectory/sensing samples and segment-level traffic metrics are now available; Weber-law curve fitting still needs digitized reference curves before quantitative fitting can be claimed.

rows.next_action: Add worker contact matrices and network-calibrated task allocation metrics.
rows.title: Dynamical models of task organization in social insect colonies
rows.gap: The model has response-threshold-like task switching and exports switch rates/contact summaries, but it still lacks calibrated worker-worker contact matrices.

rows.next_action: Calibrate resource quality against paper-specific sucrose/protein concentration, distance and trail-laying counts.
rows.title: Modulation of pheromone trail strength with food quality in Pharaoh's ant, Monomorium pharaonis
rows.gap: The simulator now has food quality and quality-weighted recruitment, but it still lacks species-specific sucrose concentration calibration and direct trail-laying event counts.

rows.next_action: Add digitized paper-level biological measurements and fit only shared model parameters, not paper-specific exception rules.
rows.title: A continuous model of ant foraging with pheromones and trail formation
